claim: WHO Mental Health Atlas 2023 reports treatment gap exceeds 75 percent in LMICs, surpasses 90 percent for severe disorders, and mental health workforce density in low-income countries averages 0.1 psychiatrists per 100000 population.
method: Reviewed Atlas chapters on treatment gap, workforce, and service availability by country income group.
limitations: Country-level reporting varies in completeness and recency.
Treatment gap estimates depend on prevalence surveys of variable quality.

claim: The 2018 Lancet Commission on Global Mental Health found task-sharing with community health workers effective for depression care in multiple LMIC trials, suggesting treatment gap measurement must account for non-specialist and informal care.

source.title: The Lancet Commission on global mental health and sustainable development

method: Reviewed Commission findings on treatment gap, task-sharing evidence, and measurement recommendations.
limitations: Task-sharing trial results may not generalize to all contexts.
Informal and traditional care quality is not standardized.
